title: MAESTRO
logline: The complex love story between legendary composer-conductor Leonard Bernstein and actress Felicia Montealegre, spanning from his meteoric rise in 1943 through their marriage, his struggles with sexuality, her illness, and the enduring bond that defined both their lives.
antagonistOrConflict: LB's internal conflict between his public persona, his marriage to Felicia, and his homosexuality; later, Felicia's terminal illness
tone: Intimate, elegiac, emotionally complex, with moments of exuberance contrasted against profound melancholy
themes: The cost of genius and ambition
The performance of self in public and private life
The tension between artistic passion and personal responsibility

actBreaks.description: Time jumps forward to 1955 as LB and Felicia are now married with children, establishing their domestic life and shifting from courtship to the complexities of their union.

validation.ruling: Scene #30 shows LB and Felicia in 1955 preparing for the Edward R. Murrow TV interview, now married with a child—this irreversibly shifts from his bachelor conductor life to public family man, fundamentally changing the story's direction and establishing the central tension of his dual identity.

actBreaks.description: The confrontation in the Dakota bedroom where Felicia directly addresses LB's affair with Tommy, marking the emotional rupture of their marriage and the beginning of the final movement.

validation.ruling: Scene #68 is the bedroom confrontation where Felicia has locked LB out and delivers her devastating 'bird full of shit' speech, calling out his behavior—this is the irreversible breaking point in their marriage that forces the story into its final act of consequences and reckoning.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality:
This script employs a sophisticated non-linear architecture that bookends the narrative with 1989 (scenes 1, 97-98), then spirals through three distinct temporal zones: the early romance (1943-1955), the established marriage under strain (1955-1971), and the final reckoning (1971-1978). The structure mirrors a musical composition—opening with a coda, then moving through movements of increasing emotional intensity. The black-and-white sequences of the early years contrast with the color of later life, creating visual punctuation for the emotional shifts.

The script resists conventional three-act rigidity by organizing around emotional watersheds rather than plot mechanics. Act One establishes LB's meteoric rise and courtship with Felicia through scene 29, culminating in their intimate conversation in the topiary maze. The transition to Act Two (scene 30) leaps forward twelve years to show the marriage in full bloom but with cracks forming. Act Two explores the mounting tension of LB's double life until the devastating confrontation in scene 68. Act Three follows Felicia's illness and death, resolving in LB's final teaching moment at Tanglewood—a circular return to artistic purpose.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deliberately episodic and elliptical, favoring emotional snapshots over continuous narrative. The opening 29 scenes move rapidly through 1943-1944, establishing LB's career breakthrough and romance with compressed intensity (averaging 1.5 pages per scene). The middle section (scenes 30-68) slows considerably, dwelling in domestic moments and mounting marital tension across the 1955-1974 period. A montage sequence (scenes 36-42) compresses years of family life into rapid cuts. The final act (scenes 69-98) adopts a more measured, contemplative rhythm as it chronicles Felicia's decline, with longer scenes allowing emotional weight to accumulate. The script's most extended sequences—the Dakota party (scene 43, 6 pages), the Gruen interview (scene 48, 3 pages), and the Ely Cathedral performance (scenes 74-77)—serve as emotional anchors in their respective movements.

incitingIncident.description: 25-year-old Leonard Bernstein receives an early morning phone call informing him that conductor Bruno Walter is ill and he must substitute at Carnegie Hall that afternoon with no rehearsal.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. APARTMENT, NEW YORK, NY - MORNING (NOVEMBER 14, 1943)

validation.ruling: Scene #2 is the phone call offering LB the Carnegie Hall debut after Bruno Walter falls ill—this shatters his ordinary world, forces immediate action, establishes his main trajectory as a conductor, and occurs at 2.8% (ideal timing for inciting incident).

midpoint.description: LB is interviewed by John Gruen who praises his accomplishments; LB dismisses his work as not much created and expresses that conducting is what he does best, revealing his internal conflict about his legacy.
midpoint.sceneHeading: EXT. POOL PATIO, FAIRFIELD HOUSE, CONNECTICUT - DAY

validation.ruling: Scene #48 is a reflective interview lacking dramatic shift. Scene #43 (p47, 43.5%) shows LB's Dakota party in 1971 where he's publicly living a double life with Tommy present—this represents his full shift from hiding his sexuality to proactive embrace of his identity, a point of no return that raises personal stakes and marks the false victory before consequences arrive.

climax.description: LB enters Felicia's room dressed as a doctor trying to be playful, but stops when he sees her condition, and she reaches for him to lie down beside her, holding his arm and telling him he smells like tuna fish and cigarettes.
climax.sceneHeading: INT. FELICIA'S ROOM, EAST HAMPTON HOUSE - SAME TIME

validation.ruling: Scene #89 shows a dog running on a sea wall, not the climax. Scene #90 (p102, 94.4%) is the ultimate confrontation: LB enters Felicia's room trying to be playful but faces her dying condition; she reaches for him and he lies beside her—this is the peak emotional confrontation resolving their relationship's central question and demonstrating his character arc.

scenes.heading: INT. HALL/LIVING ROOM, FAIRFIELD HOUSE, CONNECTICUT - DAY (1989)
scenes.summary: Leonard Bernstein, age 70, plays piano alone in his living room while being filmed by a camera crew, then pauses struggling and turns to address an interviewer.

scenes.heading: INT. APARTMENT, NEW YORK, NY - MORNING (NOVEMBER 14, 1943)
scenes.summary: 25-year-old Leonard Bernstein receives an early morning phone call informing him that conductor Bruno Walter is ill and he must substitute at Carnegie Hall that afternoon with no rehearsal.
scenes.turningPointNote: The inciting incident — LB receives the career-defining opportunity that will launch him to fame

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: LB walks through a long hallway in a daze and opens a door.

scenes.heading: INT. BALCONY BOX, CARNEGIE HALL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: LB leans over a balcony box at Carnegie Hall and the camera flies down toward the empty stage, revealing the full house with 2804 seats.

scenes.heading: INT. BACKSTAGE, CARNEGIE HALL - EARLY AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: LB walks backstage in a suit while Bruno Zirato walks alongside him; LB complains about having no rehearsal and no downbeat rest, and they argue as they reach the stage door.

scenes.heading: INT. STAGE, CARNEGIE HALL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: LB walks to the podium sweating, makes eye contact with every orchestra member, then lifts his hand with self-assurance and drives through the downbeat rest.

scenes.heading: INT. STAGE, CARNEGIE HALL - LATER
scenes.summary: Bruno Zirato addresses the standing audience after the performance, explaining that LB was called that morning and conducted without rehearsal; the audience is captivated by LB.

scenes.heading: INT. LB’S APARTMENT, NEW YORK, NY - DAY (1943)
scenes.summary: LB sits on the toilet with a score while Jerry Robbins and David Oppenheim discuss his successful debut; Jerry pushes LB to finish composing music for their ballet collaboration while LB expresses his love for the music.

scenes.heading: INT. LB’S APARTMENT, NEW YORK, NY - DAYS LATER
scenes.summary: LB and Aaron Copland play four hands at the piano while David Oppenheim records them on a disc recorder, finishing with a flourish.

scenes.heading: INT. LB’S APARTMENT, NEW YORK, NY - LATER
scenes.summary: LB gives David a foot massage while they flirt and laugh together; LB speaks into a microphone recording romantic words about David being too much for one man.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUBURBAN STREET, QUEENS, NY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Felicia Montealegre steps off a bus in Queens and walks toward a house where music and voices can be heard from inside.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM, CLAUDIO ARRAU’S HOUSE, QUEENS, NY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Felicia enters a crowded party and watches a performance; Shirley Bernstein greets her and introduces her to Betty and Adolph Comden and Adolph Green, who are performing.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. LIVING ROOM, CLAUDIO ARRAU’S HOUSE, QUEENS, NY - LATER
scenes.summary: LB and Felicia talk outside a window while Ellen Adler sings inside; LB recounts details about Felicia's background and they discuss her acting career with mutual excitement and attraction.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUBURBAN STREET, QUEENS, NY - LATER
scenes.summary: LB and Felicia laugh and race to catch a city bus together, making it just before it pulls away.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET, GREENWICH VILLAGE, NY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: LB and Felicia walk together toward the Provincetown Playhouse and disappear into the theater.

scenes.heading: INT. STAIRWAY, PROVINCETOWN PLAYHOUSE, GREENWICH VILLAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: LB and Felicia descend stairs into the theater; Felicia directs LB onto the stage and tells him to take his shoes off.

scenes.heading: INT. STAGE, PROVINCETOWN PLAYHOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: LB stands alone on stage lit by a ghost light; Felicia brings him a script of Lorca's play and they read a romantic scene together where LB's character confesses love, ending with them kissing passionately.
scenes.turningPointNote: LB and Felicia's first kiss — the beginning of their romantic relationship despite LB's homosexuality

scenes.heading: INT. STAGE, BROADWAY HOUSE, NEW YORK, NY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Felicia walks downstage in costume and takes a bow to a standing ovation from a crowded theater, receiving flowers from the cast.

scenes.heading: INT. STAGE, CARNEGIE HALL, NEW YORK, NY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: LB rises from a bow at Carnegie Hall, absorbing applause from a full house, then gestures for his orchestra to join him.

scenes.heading: INT. DRESSING ROOM, BROADWAY HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Shirley visits Felicia in her dressing room after the performance and introduces her to Dick Hart, a handsome married actor; Felicia and Dick are immediately attracted to each other.

scenes.heading: EXT. TANGLEWOOD, LENOX, MA - DAY
scenes.summary: LB and Felicia sit under a tree playing a telepathy game where she tries to send him a number; they laugh comfortably together as LB repeatedly guesses wrong.

scenes.heading: EXT. 2ND FLOOR BEDROOM/TERRACE, SERANAK HOUSE, TANGLEWOOD - DAY
scenes.summary: Felicia unpacks clothes in a bedroom and notices LB fumbling with his tie; she takes his hand, leads him to the terrace, and puts a different tie on him.

scenes.heading: INT. FOYER, SERANAK HOUSE, TANGLEWOOD - DAY
scenes.summary: LB and Serge Koussevitzky walk through the foyer following their wives toward a lunch table; Koussevitzky asks where LB met Felicia.

scenes.heading: EXT. DINING TERRACE, SERANAK HOUSE, TANGLEWOOD - LATER
scenes.summary: At lunch on the terrace, Felicia discusses her acting career while LB jokes about his terrible screen test; Aaron Copland praises LB as a beloved teacher at Tanglewood and discusses his conducting success.

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE/STAGE, ADELPHI THEATER, NEW YORK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: LB and Felicia sit in the Adelphi theater watching the Fancy Free ballet performance; Felicia asks why he would give up composing ballets, and LB questions whether it's serious music.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM, SERANAK HOUSE, TANGLEWOOD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: LB and Felicia lie half-naked on the floor post-coital, smoking; they joke about LB's breathing problems and discuss his asthma while Felicia rests her head on his shoulder.

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE/STAGE, THE SHED, TANGLEWOOD - DAY
scenes.summary: LB walks down the aisle toward David Oppenheim conducting on stage and praises his performance; David notices Felicia entering with Aaron Copland, and LB introduces Felicia to David.

scenes.heading: EXT. FIELD, BEYOND SERANAK HOUSE, TANGLEWOOD - DAY
scenes.summary: LB and Felicia walk arm in arm across a wide field away from the camera.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOPIARY MAZE, TANGLEWOOD - DAY
scenes.summary: LB and Felicia walk through a topiary maze; Felicia tells him life isn't serious and they can be free without guilt, knowing who he is; LB drops to one knee to propose and Felicia protests it's not how she meant it.
scenes.turningPointNote: LB proposes to Felicia despite his homosexuality — she accepts the arrangement, establishing the central tension of their marriage

scenes.heading: INT. HALL/STUDIO, OSBORNE APARTMENT, NEW YORK, NY - DAY (1955)
scenes.summary: LB, now 37, and Felicia, now 33, carry three-year-old Jamie through their hallway while Edward R. Murrow's voice narrates their successful careers and family life for a television profile.

scenes.heading: INT. STAIRWELL/LOBBY, OSBORNE APARTMENTS, NEW YORK, NY - DAY
scenes.summary: LB descends a carved-wood stairwell and catches a paper airplane from young Jamie with a message reading 'I love you, Jamie.'

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET BY CENTRAL PARK, NEW YORK, NY - DAY
scenes.summary: LB crosses the street to meet David Oppenheim, his wife Ellen Adler, and their newborn; LB kisses both David and Ellen on the mouth in greeting.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET ALONG CENTRAL PARK - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: LB and David walk together in silence until LB stops; David caresses LB's cheek while LB glances at people across the street staring at them, then jokes about being recognized.

scenes.heading: EXT. 2ND FLOOR TERRACE, FAIRFIELD HOUSE, CONNECTICUT - DAY
scenes.summary: LB stands on the terrace smoking while Felicia joins him; they discuss missing silverware, curtains, and their children including baby Alex who sleeps all day.

scenes.heading: EXT. YARD, FAIRFIELD HOUSE, CONNECTICUT - DAY
scenes.summary: Felicia and Shirley lounge on a quilt having a conversation about personality and self-adjustment while LB plays with young Jamie and her friend in the background.

scenes.heading: EXT. YARD, FAIRFIELD HOUSE, CONNECTICUT - DAY (MONTAGE)
scenes.summary: LB lies on the grass while Felicia and Jamie balance on his back; he lifts them in a push-up and they topple over laughing, then LB grabs Felicia's shoes and runs away.

scenes.heading: INT. SALON, FAIRFIELD HOUSE, CONNECTICUT - NIGHT (MONTAGE)
scenes.summary: LB and Felicia dance in a sweet embrace at a dinner party while guests dance around them.

scenes.heading: EXT. YARD, FAIRFIELD HOUSE, CONNECTICUT - DAY (MONTAGE)
scenes.summary: LB walks toward the camera carrying baby Alex while young Jamie and her friend play in the background; he joins Aaron Copland on a tree swing and they look at the baby together.

scenes.heading: INT. BACKSTAGE, CONCERT HALL - NIGHT (MONTAGE)
scenes.summary: Felicia stands in the wings dwarfed by LB's shadow as he conducts Mahler on stage.

scenes.heading: EXT. YARD, FAIRFIELD HOUSE - CONNECTICUT - DAY (MONTAGE)
scenes.summary: LB and Felicia dote on baby Alex in Julia Vega's arms and on Jamie, who smiles then runs away through the garden pergola.

scenes.heading: INT. STAGE, CONCERT HALL - DAY (MONTAGE)
scenes.summary: LB, now in his 40s, conducts with intense energy as the music swells.

scenes.heading: INT. BACKSTAGE, CONCERT HALL - NIGHT (MONTAGE)
scenes.summary: LB comes offstage to Felicia and crashes into her in a full embrace with a deep kiss before heading back for an encore; Felicia smokes and watches him take applause, then the scene transitions to another backstage where she watches resigned.
scenes.turningPointNote: Visual transition showing the passage of time and Felicia's growing resignation about LB's absences and affairs

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM, DAKOTA APARTMENT, NEW YORK, NY - NIGHT (1971)
scenes.summary: Felicia stands at a window during a party in their Dakota apartment; Mendy Wager discusses interior design with her while across the room LB tells Shirley he can't make sense of Felicia; LB then flirts with Tommy Cothran and they leave the party together.

scenes.heading: INT. FOYER, DAKOTA APARTMENT, NEW YORK, NY - LATER
scenes.summary: Shirley stands in the foyer looking toward where LB and Tommy disappeared; Felicia asks if she's seen Lenny and Shirley offers to fetch him but Felicia says she'll go herself.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY, THE DAKOTA, NEW YORK, NY - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: LB and Tommy exit the elevator laughing and crash against the hallway wall face to face; Tommy discusses a problem while LB's arm is around him with a cigarette in hand.

scenes.heading: INT. FOYER, DAKOTA APARTMENT, NEW YORK, NY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tommy disappears into the party as LB catches up with Felicia; she tells him to fix his hair because he's getting sloppy, then leaves him alone.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM, DAKOTA APARTMENT, NEW YORK, NY - LATER
scenes.summary: LB sits alone under the window with a drink and cigarette after the party ends while Julia Vega cleans up in the background.

scenes.heading: EXT. POOL PATIO, FAIRFIELD HOUSE, CONNECTICUT - DAY
scenes.summary: LB is interviewed by John Gruen who praises his accomplishments; LB dismisses his work as not much created and expresses that conducting is what he does best, revealing his internal conflict about his legacy.
scenes.turningPointNote: LB articulates his core artistic conflict — questioning whether he's created enough serious work versus conducting others' music

scenes.heading: INT. CHELSEA THEATER, BROOKLYN ACADEMY OF MUSIC, BROOKLYN - DAY
scenes.summary: LB rehearses the chorus for the 1973 Candide revival, conducting from his stool with joy while Felicia watches from the house; she smiles but her expression suggests underlying concern.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT YARD/DRIVEWAY, FAIRFIELD HOUSE, CONNECTICUT - DAY
scenes.summary: 20-year-old Jamie sits melancholically on a tree swing and looks up as LB, Harry Kraut, and Tommy arrive in a convertible; LB asks Tommy to get a bag of kibble from the car.

scenes.heading: EXT. POOL, FAIRFIELD HOUSE, CONNECTICUT - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Alexander and Nina greet LB with open arms as he arrives at the pool area, and someone watches from an upstairs window as the men continue inside.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN/STAIRS/HALLWAY, FAIRFIELD HOUSE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: LB and Tommy enter the kitchen while Tommy admires a painting in the hallway, and Felicia calls down to LB from the top of the stairs with something amiss.

scenes.heading: INT. MASTER BEDROOM, FAIRFIELD HOUSE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Felicia tells LB that Jamie is upset about gossip she's heard about him at Tanglewood, and LB becomes visibly upset while undressing and asking what Felicia told her.

scenes.heading: EXT. PORCH, LB’S STUDY, FAIRFIELD HOUSE, CONNECTICUT - DAY
scenes.summary: LB approaches Jamie on the porch and she greets him warmly, and he begins to address what her mother told him about her being upset.

scenes.heading: INT. DRAWING ROOM, FAIRFIELD HOUSE, CONNECTICUT - DAY
scenes.summary: Felicia paints Nina's nails while Jamie sits with them, and Tommy joins to have his nails painted as they banter about nail polish colors.

scenes.heading: EXT. POOL PATIO, FAIRFIELD HOUSE, CONNECTICUT - DAY
scenes.summary: LB sits with Felicia by the pool and tries to explain his interest in spending time with Tommy, but Felicia laughs dismissively and the conversation becomes tense and circular.

scenes.heading: INT. LENNY'S STUDY, FAIRFIELD HOUSE, CONNECTICUT - LATER
scenes.summary: LB sits alone at the piano in his study, composing and writing down musical phrases that will become part of Mass, adding vocal and instrumental parts to the score.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD, FAIRFIELD HOUSE, CONNECTICUT - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: LB walks from his study across the backyard toward the main house while music from Mass plays in his head.

scenes.heading: INT. DRAWING ROOM, FAIRFIELD HOUSE, CONNECTICUT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: LB enters the drawing room where Felicia and the kids are, announces he has finished Mass while raising the score in triumph, and Felicia suddenly jumps out the open window.
scenes.turningPointNote: Felicia's impulsive leap through the window signals a breaking point in her emotional state regarding LB's behavior and their marriage.

scenes.heading: EXT. YARD/POOL, FAIRFIELD HOUSE, CONNECTICUT - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Felicia jumps fully clothed into the pool.

scenes.heading: INT. DRAWING ROOM, FAIRFIELD HOUSE, CONNECTICUT - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: LB watches Felicia in the pool from the window as the kids rush out, his demeanor shifts, and he takes a drag and picks up his finished score.

scenes.heading: INT. POOL, FAIRFIELD HOUSE, CONNECTICUT - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Felicia lies motionless underwater at the bottom of the pool, seemingly at peace and perhaps not wanting to come back up.

scenes.heading: INT. CONCERT HALL, KENNEDY CENTER, WASHINGTON, DC - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The audience watches the end of Mass at Kennedy Center, and LB sits with Tommy, Felicia, and Harry in the balcony, where LB whispers to Tommy and squeezes his hand while Felicia observes this intimacy.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, KENNEDY CENTER, WASHINGTON, DC - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the crowded lobby after the performance, LB shakes hands and accepts congratulations, then Felicia approaches and says something unfriendly to him before walking away.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM/BEDROOM, WATERGATE HOTEL, WASHINGTON, DC - LATER
scenes.summary: Felicia places LB's pillow, pajamas, slippers, toothbrush and toothpaste outside the bedroom doors in their hotel suite and shuts the doors, locking him out.

scenes.heading: INT. FOYER/LIVING ROOM, DAKOTA APARTMENT, NEW YORK, NY - DAY
scenes.summary: LB arrives at the Dakota apartment with a stuffed Snoopy during a Thanksgiving parade, greeting the doorman and Julia Vega as kids and adults watch the parade from the windows.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM/HALLWAY, DAKOTA APARTMENT - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Jamie tells LB he's a day late to Thanksgiving and that Nina was upset, while LB deflects and greets neighbors, and Jamie insists he needs to talk to Felicia.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM, DAKOTA APARTMENT, NEW YORK, NY - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Felicia confronts LB in their bedroom about his public affair with Tommy, using a Chilean saying about living under a bird full of shit, and LB tries to minimize and deflect her anger while making himself a drink.
scenes.turningPointNote: Felicia directly confronts LB about his affair in an explosive argument that marks the collapse of their ability to maintain the facade of their marriage.

scenes.heading: INT. AVERY FISHER HALL, LINCOLN CENTER, NY - DAY (FALL 1976)
scenes.summary: LB walks onto the stage at Avery Fisher Hall for an open Thursday rehearsal, appearing heavier and bearded, and begins discussing Shostakovich's 14th Symphony with the audience.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUNDSTAGE, TELEVISION STUDIO, NEW YORK, NY - DAY
scenes.summary: Felicia and Mendy perform a reading of Facade with chamber orchestra accompaniment on a television soundstage, and after the director calls cut, the stage manager approaches them during the break.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM, BUNGALOW, LOS ANGELES, CA - DAY
scenes.summary: LB sits at a party in Los Angeles surrounded by men including Harry and Tommy, holding a serving tray of cocaine and discussing how handsome everyone looks before snorting cocaine off the tray.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM, BUNGALOW, LOS ANGELES, CA - LATER
scenes.summary: LB sits alone in a dark bathroom on the phone with Jamie, apologizing for living in hotel rooms and asking about her weekend plans while someone briefly interrupts looking for the bathroom.

scenes.heading: INT. PALM COURT, THE PLAZA HOTEL, NEW YORK, NY - DAY
scenes.summary: Felicia has lunch at the Plaza with Shirley and Jamie, deflecting compliments about a review and revealing she has a suitor, then tells Jamie she will stay at the theater on Thanksgiving so Jamie will eat with LB at the apartment.

scenes.heading: EXT. ELY CATHEDRAL, CAMBRIDGESHIRE, ENGLAND - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of Ely Cathedral lit up at night.

scenes.heading: INT. ELY CATHEDRAL, CAMBRIDGESHIRE, ENGLAND - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Inside Ely Cathedral, a massive performance of Mahler's Symphony No. 2 unfolds with 180 chorus members and 76 orchestra players, all commanded by one small conducting figure at the center.

scenes.heading: INT. STAGE, ELY CATHEDRAL, CAMBRIDGESHIRE, ENGLAND - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: LB conducts with sheer ecstasy and purity of spirit, serving as the conduit for Mahler's Symphony No. 2 as it reaches its conclusion.

scenes.heading: INT. WING, ELY CATHEDRAL, CAMBRIDGESHIRE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Felicia watches from the wing as the performance ends, the audience erupts in applause, and LB rushes offstage and collapses onto her while she tells him there's no hate in his heart, then he kisses her repeatedly before returning to the stage.
scenes.turningPointNote: This moment of reconciliation and intimacy between LB and Felicia after the Mahler performance represents a temporary healing and reconnection in their fractured relationship.

scenes.heading: INT. WAITING ROOM, DR. KRUGER’S OFFICE, NY, NY - DAY (JULY 1977)
scenes.summary: LB waits in a doctor's office waiting room with a cigarette and magazine until a nurse calls him back.

scenes.heading: INT. EXAMINATION ROOM, DR. KRUGER’S OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Felicia sits on an examination table in a gown while LB paces and holds her hand, and Dr. Kruger enters to deliver the diagnosis that Felicia has breast cancer that has metastasized to her lungs and liver.
scenes.turningPointNote: The devastating diagnosis of terminal cancer is delivered, fundamentally altering the trajectory of both characters' lives and their relationship.

scenes.heading: INT. BLACK CAR, OUTSIDE DR. KRUGER’S OFFICE, NY, NY - DAY
scenes.summary: LB helps Felicia into a car outside the doctor's office, then pauses and suggests they walk through the park instead, leading her back out into the light.

scenes.heading: EXT. CENTRAL PARK, NEW YORK, NY - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: LB and Felicia sit back-to-back on the grass in Central Park like they did years ago at Tanglewood, playing a guessing game where LB says the number is two, like the two of them.

scenes.heading: INT. SITTING ROOM, EAST HAMPTON HOUSE, LONG ISLAND, NY - DAY
scenes.summary: Felicia sits gaunt and bald in a mobcap as Cynthia and Mendy visit, and they discuss the difficult drive and whether Felicia needs anything, with Jamie checking on her from offscreen.

scenes.heading: INT. SITTING ROOM, EAST HAMPTON HOUSE, LONG ISLAND, NY - LATER
scenes.summary: LB and Felicia say goodbye to Cynthia and Mendy, but Felicia can't handle it anymore and tells LB she wants no more visitors and wants to go to bed.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM, EAST HAMPTON HOUSE, LONG ISLAND, NY - DAY
scenes.summary: LB sits on the phone telling Harry to cancel an engagement because he refuses to leave Felicia, then after hanging up he closes the door and screams into a couch cushion.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM, EAST HAMPTON HOUSE, LONG ISLAND, NY - DAY
scenes.summary: Felicia sits on a closed toilet seat carefully folding pieces of toilet paper into little rectangular pocket squares.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM, EAST HAMPTON HOUSE, LONG ISLAND, NY - LATER
scenes.summary: Jamie tries to help Felicia drink water and use the bathroom, but Felicia insists she's freezing and refuses Jamie's help, telling her to let go.

scenes.heading: INT. FELICIA’S BEDROOM, EAST HAMPTON HOUSE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Jamie helps Felicia into bed and climbs in beside her, and Felicia embraces Jamie tightly while apologizing and telling her that all anyone needs is kindness.

scenes.heading: EXT. LIVING ROOM, EAST HAMPTON HOUSE, LONG ISLAND. NY - DAY
scenes.summary: Felicia cuts LB's hair while heavily medicated and confused about which doctor prescribed her medicine, and LB kisses her hand and reminds her to take her medication after the haircut.

scenes.heading: EXT. SEA WALL, EAST HAMPTON HOUSE, LONG ISLAND, NY - DAY
scenes.summary: Julia Vega runs after Sweet Gene the dog, waving a dish towel frantically.

scenes.heading: INT. FELICIA'S ROOM, EAST HAMPTON HOUSE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: LB enters Felicia's room dressed as a doctor trying to be playful, but stops when he sees her condition, and she reaches for him to lie down beside her, holding his arm and telling him he smells like tuna fish and cigarettes.

scenes.heading: INT. FELICIA’S BATHROOM, EAST HAMPTON HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Through the bathroom window, Nina runs across the lawn followed by LB who catches her, and she buries her head in his neck crying as he carries her back toward the house with Alex joining them.
scenes.turningPointNote: This scene marks Felicia's death, as Nina's grief-stricken reaction and LB carrying her back signals the moment of loss that transforms the family.

scenes.heading: EXT. SEA WALL, EAST HAMPTON HOUSE, LONG ISLAND, NY - DAY
scenes.summary: LB stands alone at the sea wall looking out.

scenes.heading: EXT. DRIVEWAY, EAST HAMPTON HOUSE, LONG ISLAND, NY - DAY
scenes.summary: LB, the kids, and Julia Vega finish packing cars in the driveway and drive away from the East Hampton house without Felicia.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE SHED, TANGLEWOOD, LENOX, MA - DAY (LATE SUMMER 1989)
scenes.summary: A gold convertible Mercedes with license plate MAESTRO1 drives up to The Shed at Tanglewood while R.E.M.'s song mentioning LB by name plays on the radio.

scenes.heading: INT. STAGE/HOUSE, THE SHED, TANGLEWOOD - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: LB coaches a young conducting student named William on stage at Tanglewood, then watches as William conducts Beethoven's Symphony No. 8 until LB interrupts to give further instruction about the fermata and conducting technique.

scenes.heading: INT. SMALL THEATER, TANGLEWOOD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: LB dances wildly at a student party with his shirt unbuttoned, dancing close and sensually with William, then standing alone pounding his chest gorilla-style.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM, FAIRFIELD HOUSE, CONNECTICUT - DAY (1989)
scenes.summary: LB sits at the piano in his Fairfield living room addressing an interviewer, reciting a quote Felicia told him from Edna St. Vincent Millay about summer singing, then takes a drag and asks if there are any questions.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAIRFIELD HOUSE, CONNECTICUT - DAY (1955, MEMORY)
scenes.summary: In black and white, young Felicia looks directly at the camera with an enigmatic smile before turning away, followed by archival footage of the real Leonard Bernstein conducting with emotional brilliance before blowing a kiss.
